source.title: Temu Plots Aggressive US Comeback With 60% Price Cuts (BoF)

extractions.claim: Temu cut best-selling product prices by 18% on average (up to 60%) in September 2025 to rebuild US market after tariff-driven retreat
extractions.evidence: At least two dozen of Temu's best-selling products tracked by Bloomberg saw prices drop by 18% on average in early September 2025 compared with late April, with some items experiencing cuts reaching 60%. Temu eliminated import fees that previously increased checkout costs significantly. The comeback followed US sales plunging >30% in June and continuing to fall >10% in July-August.

verification.verifier_notes: Bloomberg tracking of best-seller prices, reported by BoF. Pre-SCOTUS recovery effort.

extractions.claim: Temu shifted from cross-border direct shipping to US warehouse fulfillment after de minimis elimination
extractions.evidence: Temu halted shipping products from China directly to US shoppers in May 2025 after de minimis elimination. Items shipped directly from China were labeled as out of stock. Temu shifted to only display listings for products shipped from US-based warehouses. The company is constructing a comprehensive logistics network including cross-border shipments, warehouse storage, and last-mile delivery.

verification.verifier_notes: CNBC reported the halt; BoF reported the logistics buildout. Multiple sources confirm.

extractions.claim: Pre-SCOTUS tariff surcharges on Temu items ranged 130-150%, more than doubling prices
extractions.evidence: Temu added import charges of 130-150% in April-May 2025. Examples: summer dress $18.47 -> $44.68 (142% surcharge); child's bathing suit $12.44 -> $31.12 (150% surcharge). This followed Trump's 145% tariff on Chinese imports and de minimis elimination on May 2, 2025. Daily active users dropped 58% in US. App ranking fell from top-5 to 75th in Apple Store.

verification.verifier_notes: CNBC, CNN, ABC News all reported identical surcharge data. Multiple independent confirmations.
